TimeEntry.Title: Monthly Switch Backup for ISI Switch:-192.168.1.132
TimeEntry.Notes: Accessed ISI Switch:-192.168.1.132 switch using putty and performed configuration backup. placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network- config-Switch folder. Hence, closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: ISI Servers pending for reboot & updates after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machines are now in healthy systems.

ISI-HQ-AD-01, ISI-HQ-APP-01, ISI-HQ-DB-01, ISI-HQ-VBR-01, ISI-HQ-VONE

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - ISI-HQ-VBR-01.isi.local: (192.168.1.20) - Disk Utilization is 80%, threshold value for this monitor is 70%
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the alert connected to ISI-HQ-VBR-01 download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

TimeEntry.Title: WKISIFD01.isi.local, ISI-HQ-DB-01, newwkisi5.isi.local : Found critical error, fixed it
TimeEntry.Notes:
Systems Accessed:
WKISIFD01.isi.local
ISI-HQ-DB-01
newwkisi5.isi.local

Issue:
Sync errors reported on the listed machines.

Action Taken:
Accessed each system, reviewed the reported errors, and performed the required troubleshooting and fixes.

Resolution:
All identified errors were resolved successfully.

Verification:
Confirmed that all machines are now syncing with the server without any alarms.

Status:
Issue resolved.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: WKISIFD01,WKISI01 : Update cisco umbrella with latest version
TimeEntry.Notes:
WKISIFD01, WKISI01:
I accessed both machines, removed the older version, and downloaded and installed the latest version of Cisco Secure Client (cisco-secure-client-win-5.1.14.145). I also verified in the portal that both systems are reflecting the latest version and are syncing properly.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Sharon with ISI having issue with Network Shared Drive...
TimeEntry.Notes:
Sharon called in regarding the network Shared Drive issue....

so connected to the system and checked she is getting permission issue while access the network drive....

so restart the computer and tuned off the firewall settings...

Also remove the old credentails from Credentials manager...

she is able to access the Drive ok....hence closing the ticket.....

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20505.0)updated on machines ISI-HQ-AD-01,ISI-HQ-APP-01,ISI-HQ-DB-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines ISI-HQ-AD-01,ISI-HQ-APP-01,ISI-HQ-DB-01
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.33.20505.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam One application configuration error.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into ISI-HQ-VONE server and then go through the errors on the Veeam one console and then troubleshoot the errors. Re-setup the inventor and update the connection with esxi servers for client. Once the connection was established then update the alarms again on VONE.

TimeEntry.Title: Server ISI-HQ-DB-01,ISI-HQ-AD-01 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan the machines are now in healthy systems.

Machine :ISI-HQ-DB-01,ISI-HQ-AD-01

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on ISI Servers
TimeEntry.Notes: Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine ISI-HQ-AD-01, ISI-HQ-APP-01, ISI-HQ-VBR-01, ISI-HQ-VONE, WKISI01running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  . Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required -ISI-HQ-DB-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server ISI-HQ-DB-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: ISI-HQ-VONE,newwkisi5 : Found cisco umbrella old version, update with latest (5.1.14.145)
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed both machines, downloaded the latest package (Cisco Secure Client 5.1.14.145), completed the update successfully.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required - ISI-HQ-AD-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to ESXI and found the server ISI-HQ-AD-01 is in Disk consolidation state.checked the snapshots and started consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully.Then logged into the server and verified all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Server restart after APD ISI-HQ-VBR-01 & ISI-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
After installing the Windows updates, the servers required a restart.

Restarted ISI-HQ-VBR-01 (Veeam Backup Server) and ISI-HQ-VONE (Veeam ONE Server). After reboot, checked the Veeam backup services and found that the backup backup services were not running.

Attempted initial troubleshooting; however, the issue was not resolved.

Assigned the ticket to the next shift for further investigation.

Installed Updates:

  • 2026-02 Cumulative Update for Windows Server 2019 (1809) for x64-based Systems (KB5075904)

  • Security Intelligence Update for Microsoft Defender Antivirus – KB2267602 (Version 1.445.179.0) – Current Channel (Broad)

  • 2021-01 Update for Windows Server 2019 for x64-based Systems (KB4589208)

TimeEntry.Title: Server restart after APD ISI-HQ-VBR-01 & ISI-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the handover received from the previous shift regarding the Veeam Backup service failing to start (which prevented access to the Veeam console), the issue was investigated. It was identified that the PostgreSQL patch was missing and the PostgreSQL installation on the server was corrupted.

The PostgreSQL setup was downloaded and repaired. After completing the repair, the server was rebooted. Post-reboot, all required services were verified and confirmed to be running successfully.

Logged into the Veeam Backup & Replication console and restarted the backup jobs. The backups are now passed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20504.0)updated on machines ISI-HQ-VONE, WKISI01, WKISIFD01,NEWWKISI5
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines ISI-HQ-VONE, WKISI01, WKISIFD01,NEWWKISI5 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines from backend and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.33.20504.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on ISI-HQ-AD-01,ISI-HQ-APP-01,ISI-HQ-DB-01,ISI-HQ-VBR-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on  is old version V0.14.96. Connected to the machines
ISI-HQ-AD-01,ISI-HQ-APP-01,ISI-HQ-DB-01,ISI-HQ-VBR-01 from manage engine, download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.132. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required - ISI-HQ-APP-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server ISI-HQ-APP-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Missing Patches are Successfully Retried/Deployed on ISI-HQ-DB-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Missing Patches are Successfully Retried/Deployed on ISI-HQ-DB-01

Now the Vulnerability Status is HEALTHY
